skill.name: browser-act
skill.description: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Enables navigation, content extraction, screenshots, form automation, session management, and network capture through a full browser engine.
skill.summary: Full browser engine for AI-driven web automation, content extraction, and multi-session management
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 11 potential issues: 5 markdown backtick patterns misinterpreted as Ruby shell execution, 1 legitimate homepage URL flagged as hardcoded URL, 2 mentions of screenshot capability flagged as data exfiltration, and 3 false weak-cryptographic-algorithm detections with no evidence in file contents. After AI evaluation, all 11 findings are false positives. The skill is a browser automation CLI with transparent permission declarations, explicit local-only data privacy policy, and Confirmation Gate user approval protocol. No malicious intent or exfiltration patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks misidentified as Ruby shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 5 instances of backtick-delimited text in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All 5 instances are standard markdown inline code formatting or fenced code blocks. Line 41 contains an install command in inline code. Line 45 contains the tool name in inline code. Lines 47-49 are a fenced bash code block. Line 57 contains a command reference in inline code. None execute shell commands via Ruby backticks. False positive.

low_findings.title: Homepage URL in metadata flagged as hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged https://www.browseract.com at line 9 as a hardcoded network URL. This is the legitimate project homepage URL in the YAML front matter metadata field. No malicious endpoint. False positive.

low_findings.title: Screenshot capability misidentified as data exfiltration
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged mentions of screenshots at lines 3 and 36 as screen capture upload. The skill explicitly declares screenshot capability as a feature with local-only processing in its data-privacy section (line 17). All data is stored and processed locally, never uploaded. False positive.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm not present in file
low_findings.description: Static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm at lines 3 and 57. Review of SKILL.md content shows no cryptographic algorithm references, encoding patterns, or hash function usage at these locations. The description mentions HAR (HTTP Archive format) which may have triggered a pattern match. No evidence of any cryptographic operation in the file. False positive.

content.value_statement: AI agents cannot access JavaScript-rendered web pages using standard fetch tools. Browser-act provides a complete browser engine so AI can navigate, extract data, fill forms, and take screenshots from any website.
actual_capabilities: Extract rendered content from JavaScript-heavy websites
Manage multiple isolated browser sessions in parallel
Take screenshots and capture network traffic including XHR, fetch, and HAR responses
Fill forms, click elements, and upload files automatically
Handle verification challenges with user-assisted resolution
Navigate multi-step workflows with session authentication
limitations: Requires Python 3.12 and the uv package manager for installation
Cannot bypass advanced CAPTCHA systems without user intervention
Browser startup adds latency compared to simple HTTP fetch requests
Some websites may detect and block automated browser access

use_cases.title: Debug Rendered Pages
use_cases.description: Extract and inspect JavaScript-rendered DOM content that standard HTTP fetch tools cannot access

use_cases.title: Scrape Dynamic Content
use_cases.description: Collect data from dashboards and tables that load content dynamically after page load

use_cases.title: Automate UI Workflows
use_cases.description: Run multi-step form submissions, login flows, and navigation sequences across different browser sessions

prompt_templates.title: Extract a Web Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-act to open the URL and extract all visible text content from the page.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to read content from a JavaScript-heavy website

prompt_templates.title: Take a Screenshot
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-act to open the URL, wait for full page rendering, and capture a full-page screenshot.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs a visual record of a web page layout

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Form Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-act to log in, fill a search form, click the search button, and extract the results table from the results page.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs to automate a multi-step interaction on a web application

prompt_templates.title: Parallel Session Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-act to create two separate browser sessions. Open the admin dashboard in session A and the public site in session B. Extract content from both simultaneously.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs to compare content across authenticated and public views of a website

output_examples.input: Extract the main content from a JavaScript-rendered news article
output_examples.output: Opened https://news-site.com/article. Page loaded with JavaScript enabled. Extracted article title: Latest Technology Trends, body text: 1,245 words, metadata: published 2026-05-20 by Jane Doe.

output_examples.input: Take a screenshot of the dashboard and verify the page title
output_examples.output: Navigated to dashboard URL. Waited for all charts to load (3 async requests complete). Captured full-page screenshot saved to session data. Page title: Analytics Dashboard - Q2 Report 2026.

best_practices: Always create a browser session with browser-act browser create before running operations
Close browser sessions with browser-act browser close after completing all tasks
Use the verification assistance feature when websites present interactive challenges
anti_patterns: Do not skip the get-skills core step before running commands -- it contains critical environment state
Do not run multiple unrelated operations in one session without checking session health
Do not ignore the Confirmation Gate protocol -- always wait for explicit user approval

faq.question: Which AI tools work with browser-act?
faq.answer: Browser-act works with Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Windsurf, and any tool that supports shell command execution.

faq.question: Does browser-act upload my browsing data?
faq.answer: No. All cookies, login sessions, page content, credentials, and browser profile data are stored and processed locally on your machine.

faq.question: What are the system requirements?
faq.answer: Python 3.12 or later, the uv package manager, and Google Chrome installed on your system.

faq.question: How does browser-act handle login credentials?
faq.answer: Sessions are isolated per browser profile and stored locally. The Confirmation Gate requires explicit user approval before any credential submission.

faq.question: Can browser-act bypass CAPTCHA challenges?
faq.answer: Browser-act can present verification challenges to the human user for assistance. It cannot automatically solve CAPTCHA systems.

faq.question: How many browser sessions can run at once?
faq.answer: Browser-act supports multiple parallel browser sessions with independent profiles for multi-account and multi-site operations.
